input.background: An important historical case-control completed by Doll and Hill found that 647 of the 649 lung cancer cases were smokers. In contrast, 622 of 649 controls smoked.
input.question: Include a $95 \%$ confidence interval for the $O R$.

output.answer:
Determining the confidence interval for the $O R$ :

$$
\ln (14.04)=2.6419
$$
$$
S E=\operatorname{sqrt}\left(647^{-1}+622^{-1}+2^{-1}+27^{-1}\right)=0.7350
$$

The $95 \% \mathrm{CI}$ for $\mathrm{OR}=e^{(2.6419 \pm 1.96 \times 0.7350)}=e^{(1.2013,4.0825)}=3.3$ to 59.3

lung_cancer_cases_smokers.description: Number of lung cancer cases who were smokers.

lung_cancer_cases_total.description: Total number of lung cancer cases.

control_smokers.description: Number of control subjects who were smokers.
